Operation 'createSecureQRCode' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks clarity on authentication requirements, return value structure, and operational outcomes. Enhanced summary emphasizes key security attributes (digitally-signed, verifiable, tamper-proof) for better AI agent understanding. Improved description adds: (1) authentication requirement context, (2) explicit return value information (CreateQRCodeResponse), (3) side effects (QR code availability for retrieval/embedding), and (4) domain-appropriate terminology consistent with API context. This helps AI agents better determine when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Generate a secure Qryptal QR code (PDC, EDC, or HDC type). The data and its corresponding format must be supplied. For EDC/HDC types with attachments, use multipart/form-data content type.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate a secure, digitally-signed QR code
data.description_suggestion: Generates a secure, verifiable, tamper-proof Qryptal QR code in PDC, EDC, or HDC type. Requires authentication via token. Input must include data payload and corresponding format specification. For EDC/HDC types with file attachments, use multipart/form-data content type. Returns a CreateQRCodeResponse object containing the generated QR code with verification details. The created QR code is immediately available for retrieval and embedding in documents.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on authentication requirements, return value structure, and operational outcomes. Enhanced summary emphasizes key security attributes (digitally-signed, verifiable, tamper-proof) for better AI agent understanding.
